fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of assembly document the record represents in assemble-to-order scenarios.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number assigned to the assembly order from the number series that you set up in the Assembly Setup window.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the assembly item.

fields.name: Search Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies text to search for in an assembly order. For example, you could use this field to search for an assembly order when you do not know the assembly order number.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies information in addition to the description.

fields.name: Creation Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which you create the assembly order.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip:
Specifies when the assembly order was last modified.
When you change information on the assembly order, the Last Date Modified field is automatically updated to show the current system date.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item that is being assembled with the assembly order.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the item variant of the item that is being assembled.

fields.name: Inventory Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the inventory account in the general ledger to which transactions that include this assembly item are posted. The value is copied from the item card.
Additional Information
When you post transactions for this item, this code is used with a posting group code in the Inventory Posting Setup window. The accounts in which the postings for sales, discounts, and so on are made, the VAT rate, and the VAT calculation type are determined in the Inventory Posting Setup window.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the general product account in the general ledger to which transactions involving this assembly item are posted. It is copied from the item card.
Additional Information
When you post transactions for this item, this code is used with a posting group code in the Inventory Posting Setup window. The accounts in which the postings for sales, discounts, and so on are made, are determined in the Inventory Posting Setup window. The VAT rate and the VAT calculation type are also determined in this window.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the General Business Posting Group that applies to the entry.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if a comment exists for the assembly order.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location to which you want to post output of the assembly item.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the assembly order is posted.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the assembled item is due to be available for use.

fields.name: Starting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the assembly order is expected to start.

fields.name: Ending Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the assembly order is expected to finish.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bin the assembly item is posted to as output and from where it is taken to storage or shipped if it is assembled to a sales order.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the assembly item that you expect to assemble with the assembly order.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the expected order quantity, which is stated in the base unit of measure.

Additional Information

The value in the Quantity to Consume (Base) on the assembly order line is calculated by multiplying the value in the Quantity per on the assembly order line with the value in the Quantity (Base) field.

fields.name: Remaining Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the assembly item remain to be posted as assembled output.

fields.name: Remaining Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the remaining quantity, which is stated in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Assembled Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the assembly item are posted as assembled output.

fields.name: Assembled Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the assembled quantity, which is stated in the base unit of measure.

fields.name: Quantity to Assemble
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many of the assembly item units you want to partially post. To post the full quantity on the assembly order, leave the field unchanged.

fields.name: Quantity to Assemble (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity to assemble in the base unit of measure.
For more information, see Quantity to Assemble.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the assembly item are reserved for this assembly order header.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many assembly items, which are stated in the base unit of measure, are reserved for this assembly order header.

fields.name: Planning Flexibility
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the supply represented by the assembly order is considered by the planning system when calculating action messages.

fields.name: MPS Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the assembly order is a Master Planning Schedule (MPS) order and is therefore linked to a production forecast or a sales order by the planning system.

fields.name: Assemble to Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the assembly order is linked to a sales order, which indicates that the item is assembled to order.

fields.name: Posting No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number from the number series that is assigned when the assembly order is posted. It is automatically assigned from the number series that you set up in the Posted Assembly Order Nos. field.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Cost Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total unit cost of the assembly order.

fields.name: Rolled-up Assembly Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the sum of cost amounts in the assembly order lines that is rolled up to the assembly item when you use the Update Unit Cost function.
The field is used to calculate the Unit Cost field on assembly order headers as follows. All fields are on the assembly order header.
unit cost = (rolled-up assembly cost ÷ quantity) × (indirect cost percentage ÷ 100) + overhead rate

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip: Specifies the indirect cost of the assembly item as an absolute amount.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure code of the assembly item.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity per unit of measure of the assembly item on the assembly order header.
The value is copied from the Qty. of Base Unit of Measure per Unit field that is defined for the items unit of measure.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the number series that is used to assign numbers in the No. field on the assembly order header.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the number series that was used to assign a number to the assembly order from which this posted assembly order originates. The value is copied from the No. field on the assembly order header during posting.

fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the document is open, waiting to be approved, invoiced for prepayment, or released to the next stage of processing.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Assigned User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.
